An honest read on every boat we sell.

Toronto Yacht Club Brokerage operates from three Ontario marinas and the same shop floors that prep boats for paying customers. When we list a boat, we know what we're looking at.

Sell your boat Looking for a boat?
Why List With Us

Three things no one else in Ontario offers together.

On the docks where the boats are.

Krates Marina in Keswick, Driftwood Cove in Port Severn, and Grand Harbour in Brechin. Plus mobile service across the Toronto area. We see the boats every season, in and out of the water.

We prep every listing ourselves.

A full detail before the first photo. Hull, gel coat, canvas, cosmetics. The same work we do for paying customers, done on the boat we're selling for you.

Plain commission, costs at cost.

10% commission, paid by the seller from the proceeds at close. Lien searches, registration fees, and third-party reports are passed through at cost. You see the receipts.

How It Works

How we sell your boat, start to finish.

1

Listing call

We walk the boat with you. Honest pricing, honest condition, honest timing. If the boat isn't ready to list or the price won't move it, we say so.

2

Prep

Interior and exterior detail. The boat shows the way it should before anyone sees a photo. We walk through costs and what's included on the listing call.

3

Listing assets

Honest photos, wide and detail. A written walkthrough covering hull, gel coat, canvas, cosmetics, and structure. For select listings, professional drone video and a cinematic walkthrough.

4

Marketing

We put the boat in front of our network: past customers, marina contacts, and buyers who come to us directly.

5

The deal

We handle offers, the survey period, the paperwork, and the registration transfer. You sign the bill of sale and collect the proceeds.

What we tell you, and what we back up.

We are a working marine shop, not just a broker. We know boats inside and out: hull, gel coat, canvas, structure, cosmetics, and the mechanical side too. We know engines, and we know the surveyors and mechanics worth calling in this region.

On every boat we sell, the engine and mechanical systems are confirmed by an independent marine survey, with a mechanic of the buyer's choosing. Not because we cannot read an engine, but because an independent check is what protects both sides and makes a deal hold up.

We would rather a deal stand on its own than oversell a boat and burn the relationship.

Where We Operate

Three home marinas, plus mobile across Toronto.

The brokerage operates from the same marinas as the shop. If your boat is slipped at one of ours, we're already there. If it's elsewhere on Lake Simcoe, Georgian Bay, or in the GTA, we come to you.

Ready To Talk?

Two ways to start.

Get a free valuation

Tell us about your boat. You'll hear back personally with an honest price range and what it would take to list.

Request a valuation

Looking for a specific boat?

Tell us what you want. We'll let you know when something comes in, and we'll keep an eye out across our network.

Buyer intake

Sell your boat with the team that knows it.

Whether your boat is slipped at one of our marinas or somewhere else in Ontario, we'll prep it, list it, and sell it. Tell us about the boat and you'll hear back personally.

  • 10% commission, paid from the proceeds at close.
  • Prep tailored to the boat, agreed on the first call: detailing, photos, and a written walkthrough. Drone video on select listings.
  • Marketed to our network of past customers, marina contacts, and direct buyers.

We walk through the full process and any costs on the first call.

Tell us about the boat.

Fields marked with ● are required.

You'll hear back personally.

Not ready to commit to listing? Request a free valuation first. Same form, lower commitment, just a price range and a conversation.

Tell us what you're looking for.

We list boats we know personally, and we keep an eye on the wider Ontario market through our marina network. If you tell us what you want, we'll let you know when it shows up.

How This Works

A buyer's broker, not a salesperson.

You tell us the boat.

Type, length, year range, budget, where you'd want it slipped. The more specific, the better.

We surface matches.

From our current listings, from boats we know are coming to market, and from our network across Lake Simcoe, Georgian Bay, and the GTA.

You get an honest read.

When a match comes up, we tell you what we know about the boat, what we don't, and what to ask the seller's broker. We're working for you on the buy side, not flipping inventory.

What are you looking for?

Fields marked with ● are required.

You'll hear back personally.

What's your boat worth?

A free, no-commitment valuation. Tell us about the boat and you'll hear back personally with a price range and what it would take to list, if you decide to.

How This Works

A real person, not an instant number.

No instant number.

Boat pricing has too many variables to spit out a number from a form. We do this the right way: a real person, looking at real details.

No commitment.

A valuation isn't a listing. We'll tell you what we'd price the boat at and why. If you want to list with us after, great. If not, no follow-up emails, no pressure.

Handled personally.

Every valuation is done by hand, by us. No bots, no instant number. In season we get to everyone as fast as we can.

Tell us about the boat.

Fields marked with ● are required.

You'll hear back personally.

Brokerage  ›  Listings  ›  2012 Bayliner 215 Bowrider

2012 Bayliner 215 Bowrider

21 ft 6 in · Georgian Bay · Listing #TYC-001
$24,900

About this boat

A clean 2012 Bayliner 215 bowrider. A roomy, easy to run day boat that suits Georgian Bay and Lake Simcoe equally well. Powered by a MerCruiser 4.3L MPI (220 hp) on an Alpha One drive, with a full width bimini, walk through windshield, and open bow and cockpit seating for the whole family.

Owner history and what is included (engine hours, trailer, service records) added after our walkthrough.

Specifications

Year2012
MakeBayliner
Model215 Bowrider
Length21 ft 6 in
Beam8 ft 6 in (approx)
Engine4.3L MerCruiser MPI (220 hp)
Engine hoursconfirm with owner
DriveAlpha One
Hull materialFiberglass
LocationGeorgian Bay (confirm)

What we can speak to

Condition notes are finalized after we walk the boat. The notes below are a first read from current photos.

Hull, gel coat & cosmetics

Presents clean and well kept in current photos. White topsides and dark accent stripe in good cosmetic order. Final grade and any blemishes confirmed at walkthrough.

Canvas & top

Full width bimini top present and in good shape in current photos. Any additional cover or canvas confirmed at walkthrough.

Upholstery

Bow and cockpit upholstery present clean in current photos. Wear points confirmed at walkthrough.

What the buyer's survey will cover

Engines & mechanical

We know boats mechanically, and we are glad to point you to a surveyor and mechanic we trust. The engine, sterndrives, and mechanical systems are confirmed by an independent marine survey, with a mechanic of your choosing. An independent check protects both sides and keeps the deal clean.

Brokerage  ›  Listings  ›  1998 Sea Ray Sundancer 370

1998 Sea Ray Sundancer 370

37 ft · Toronto, ON · Listing #TYC-002
$98,000

About this boat

A 1998 Sea Ray Sundancer 370, a roomy twin-engine express cruiser kept in Toronto. Full camper canvas and radar arch, with the cabin accommodations these boats are known for: a real galley, an enclosed head, and room to spend the weekend aboard.

Owner history and what is included (engine hours, service records, electronics) added after our walkthrough.

Full camper canvas and enclosure present and looks intact in current photos. Confirmed at walkthrough.

Interior

Cabin, galley, and head to be photographed and noted at walkthrough.

What the buyer's survey will cover
